ogTitle: Peppermint Grove vs Perenjori: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Peppermint Grove and Perenjori.
heading: Peppermint Grove vs Perenjori — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Peppermint Grove, Western Australia and Perenjori, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Perenjori has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Peppermint Grove (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Peppermint Grove or Perenjori safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Peppermint Grove has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Perenjori scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Peppermint Grove scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Perenjori scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Peppermint Grove or Perenjori?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Perenjori has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
